content.description:
Sortable lists provide drag-to-reorder functionality for item attachments displayed in a linear list (as opposed to the spatial canvas layout).

## Concept

While the [spatial windowing system](item://ef793c27-2d4b-4c99-b05a-2769db5bc5a9) arranges children on a 2D canvas, some contexts benefit from an ordered list:

- Todo lists where order represents priority
- Document outlines where order represents sequence
- Playlists, reading lists, step-by-step instructions

Sortable lists render attachments in array order with drag handles for reordering.

## Implementation

The reorder operation modifies the parent item's `attachments` array:

1. User drags an item to a new position
2. The attachment is removed from its current index
3. Inserted at the target index
4. Parent item saved with `api.update()` to reflect new order

The `attachments` array is ordered — array position determines display order.

## Drag Interaction

- Drag handle (grip icon) on each list item
- Visual feedback: dragged item lifts, gap appears at drop target
- Drop saves the new order immediately
- Uses [silent updates](item://c0c0c0c0-0010-0000-0000-000000000000) during drag for smooth interaction

## See Also

- [Spatial Windowing](item://ef793c27-2d4b-4c99-b05a-2769db5bc5a9) — The 2D alternative to linear lists
- [Attachments](item://c0c0c0c0-0005-0000-0000-000000000000) — Ordered attachment arrays
content.hob:
;; Sortable List View — drag-and-drop reorderable list

(let [attachments (or (:attachments item) [])
      default-view-id "a1b2c3d4-e5f6-4a7b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d"]

  [:div.sortable-list-view {:style "display: flex; flex-direction: column; height: 100%; background: var(--color-bg-surface-alt);"}
    [:div.sortable-list-items
      {:style "flex: 1; overflow-y: auto; padding-left: 2px; padding-top: 4px; padding-bottom: 10px; padding-right: 2px;"
       :sortable {:handle ".drag-handle"
                  :on-reorder (fn [from to] (reorder-attachments! from to))}}
      (if (seq attachments)
        (for [child (seq attachments)]
          (let [child-id (if (string? child) child (:id child))
                view-id (or (get-in child [:view :type]) default-view-id)]
            [:div.sortable-list-item {:data-item-id child-id
                                      :data-sort-key child-id
                                      :style "display: flex; align-items: center; margin-bottom: 4px;"}
              [:div.drag-handle {:style "width: 24px; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; cursor: grab; user-select: none; flex-shrink: 0; color: var(--color-border-dark);"
                                 :title "Drag to reorder"}
                "≡"]
              [:div {:style "flex: 1; padding: 2px 4px; min-width: 0;"}
                (render-item child-id view-id)]
              [:div {:style "width: 24px; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; flex-shrink: 0;"}
                [:button {:style "background: none; border: none; color: var(--color-border-dark); cursor: pointer; font-size: 1.125rem; padding: 4px;"
                          :title "Remove from list"
                          :on-click (fn [e] (detach! child-id) (rerender!))}
                  "×"]]]))
        [:div {:style "text-align: center; color: var(--color-border-dark); padding: 60px 20px; font-style: italic;"}
          "This list is empty."])]])
